sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root לָאַךְ to depute which see).

(1) one sent, a messenger, whether from a private person, Job 1:14, or of a king, 1Sa 16:19 19:11 , 14 1Sa 19:14, 20 1Sa 19:20 1Ki 19:2, etc. (Syr. ܡܰܠܰܟܳܐ, Arab. مَلْأكُ id.)

(2) a messenger of God, i.e.-(a) an angel, Exo 23:20 33:2 2Sa 24:16 Job 33:23 (see לוּץ ); Zec 1:9, seq.; 2:2 , Zec 2:7 4:1 seq.; more fully מַלְאַךְ יְיָ Gen 16:7 21:17 22:11 , 15 Gen 22:15 Num 22:22, seqq.; Jud. 6:11 , seqq.; Cf. De Angelologia V. T., De Wettii Bibl. Dogm. § 171, seqq. edit. 2.-(b) a prophet, Hag 1:13 Mal 3:1.-(c) a priest, Ecc 5:5 Mal 2:7. Once-(d) of Israel, as being the messenger of God and the teacher of the Gentiles, Isa 42:19. [But this passage speaks of Christ himself.]

[“מַלְאַךְ Ch. an angel; with suff. מַלְאֲכֵהּ Dan 3:28 6:23 .”]
